title: 'This is election bill': Rahul flays BJP over Rs 993 LPG price jump, warns petrol & diesel next
content: Rahul Gandhi has sharply criticized the Centre over rising inflation, highlighting a significant Rs 993 jump in commercial LPG cylinder prices in a single day. He warned that this surge, amounting to an 81% increase over three months, will heavily impact small businesses and household expenses, predicting further hikes in petrol and diesel prices.

title: Vaibhav Sooryavanshi poised for yet another record, one big hit away from 100 sixes
content: Rajasthan Royals' 15-year-old sensation Vaibhav Sooryavanshi is on the verge of a major T20 milestone, needing just one six to reach 100 career sixes. He has already achieved several remarkable records this IPL season, including the fastest 400 runs and youngest to 1,000 T20 runs.

title: Watch: Supriya Sule stuck in massive Mumbai–Pune Expressway jam, misses Maharashtra Day event
content: NCP MP Supriya Sule faced significant traffic jams while traveling to Pune. She was stuck for nearly two hours, highlighting the daily struggles of commuters. Earlier, she experienced a long wait for a cab in Mumbai, facing refusals and limited availability. These incidents underscore persistent issues with last-mile connectivity and ride availability in the city.

title: After 30k+ layoffs, AWS CEO has a message for software engineers
content: AWS CEO Matt Garman says Amazon is hiring 11,000 software development engineer interns and early-career hires in 2026, pushing back on fears that AI is eliminating software engineering jobs. His comments come after Amazon's two biggest layoff rounds in six months—30,000-plus corporate cuts in total—and amid warnings from other tech leaders that AI coding tools are fundamentally disrupting the profession. Garman acknowledges the role is changing, but insists demand for developers is accelerating.

title: Gill’s ‘Play Hold’ Dig: GT captain mocks RCB slogan after win; rivalry intensifies online
content: Shubman Gill's cryptic Instagram post, playing on RCB's slogan "Play Bold" after Gujarat Titans' victory, quickly went viral. GT secured a four-wicket win, avenging an earlier defeat and continuing their late-season surge. The win was marked by a disciplined bowling effort and crucial batting performances.

title: More work, less security: The hidden divide in India's job story
content: While India's workforce participation has increased, a deeper look reveals significant disparities. More people are working, but many are in self-employment or informal roles with uncertain incomes. Gender and location heavily influence access to stable, well-paying jobs, highlighting a critical imbalance in job quality and opportunity.

title: Indian student dies by suicide in Chicago, leaves scheduled messages for friends
content: An Indian-origin student from Andhra Pradesh, Chandu Iraganaboyina, studying in Chicago, has allegedly died by suicide. Police are investigating how he obtained a firearm on a non-immigrant visa, with friends and family citing severe financial stress as a potential cause. The Telugu community in the US has initiated a fundraiser to repatriate his body.

title: CEO says ‘When Google CEO agrees with Elon, everyone must pay attention’; Musk responds
content: Elon Musk agreed with a post highlighting Google CEO Sundar Pichai's vision of space-based data centers becoming "the new normal" within a decade. This aligns with Musk's own long-held views on utilizing space for large-scale infrastructure. Pichai previously mentioned Google's "moon shot" to place data centers in orbit to harness solar energy, with a first step planned for 2027.

title: 'Life jackets given after boat started sinking': What led to Jabalpur tragedy that killed 9
content: A cruise boat operated by the Madhya Pradesh tourism department capsized in Bargi Dam near Jabalpur, resulting in at least nine deaths. The vessel, carrying 43 passengers, overturned during a sudden, severe storm with high winds. Survivors allege life jackets were distributed only after the boat began sinking, raising questions about safety protocols.

title: 'Dhurandhar 2' BO day 44: Ranveer Singh film eyes 9-year global record of 'Baahubali 2'
content: ‘Dhurandhar 2: The Revenge’ is rewriting the record books as it nears the end of its seventh week. Having already become the first Hindi film to join the Rs. 1,000 crore net club in India, the film’s total domestic net now stands at a staggering Rs. 1,135.25 crore. On Day 44 of its theatrical run, live tracking shows the film maintaining a steady grip on the audience despite a reduced screen count. With a current worldwide gross of Rs. 1,783.90 crore, the Ranveer Singh-starrer is just steps away from overtaking the lifetime global collections of Baahubali 2.

title: Steve Jobs’ 2011 resignation letter surfaces online after Tim Cook resigns as Apple CEO
content: Apple announces leadership changes. Tim Cook will transition to executive chairman of the board. John Ternus, senior vice president of Hardware Engineering, will assume the role of Chief Executive Officer. This transition is effective September 1. Steve Jobs' 2011 resignation letter has resurfaced. Cook expressed confidence in Ternus's leadership. Ternus is grateful for the opportunity to lead Apple forward.

title: Virat Kohli ignores fan’s autograph request; child’s reaction teaches parents a lesson
content: A viral clip shows a young fan crying after Virat Kohli walks past without signing his bat. The incident sparked online debate about celebrity interaction, childhood disappointment, and emotional regulation. The moment highlights the challenges parents face in teaching resilience through everyday let-downs.

title: 'God wanted Mojtaba preserved': Iran calls US claims on Supreme Leader 'trick used by enemy'
content: An Iranian official dismissed U.S. claims about Supreme Leader Mojtaba Khamenei's health, stating he is in perfect health and actively managing state affairs. The official confirmed Khamenei survived a bombing, sustaining injuries but being preserved by divine will. This contradicts U.S. media reports alleging critical medical challenges and limited engagement following the attack.

title: Watch: J&K CM Omar Abdullah shares video of bears ‘stealing’ flag at Srinagar golf course
content: An unusual sight unfolded at Srinagar's Royal Spring Golf Course. Two bears entered the premises and made off with a flag. Jammu and Kashmir Chief Minister Omar Abdullah shared a video of the incident online. The bears' playful act amused onlookers. The golf course's proximity to forests often leads to wildlife sightings.

title: Holder catch controversy: 'Sufficient evidence for not out' - Bishop on Patidar wicket
content: Former cricketer Ian Bishop believes Rajat Patidar should have been ruled not out after Jason Holder's controversial catch. Bishop cited insufficient control and doubt about the ball touching the ground, suggesting the umpire should have reviewed more closely. Patidar's dismissal significantly impacted RCB's innings.

title: Top Iranian leaders push to remove FM Abbas Araghchi over alleged allegiance to IRGC
content: For the first time in 23 years, the U.S. has three aircraft carriers in the Middle East, accompanied by 12 ships and 15,000 soldiers. This significant military presence signals readiness for renewed conflict should the fragile ceasefire with Iran collapse. The deployment occurs amid ongoing diplomatic tensions and Iran's reblocking of the Strait of Hormuz.

title: Will not contest a Pawar family member again, says Baramati MP Supriya Sule
content: Baramati MP Supriya Sule announced she will not contest elections against any Pawar family member in the future, opting for a different constituency if necessary. Her sister-in-law, Sunetra Pawar, welcomed the decision, emphasizing the importance of separating politics and family relations. This comes after two instances of Pawar family members contesting against each other.

title: ACP-rank officer arrested for 'flashing' at 9-year-old girl in Mumbai
content: A senior police officer in Mumbai faces serious charges after allegedly exposing himself to a nine-year-old girl in a Worli garden. Arrested following a complaint, the assistant commissioner of police has been booked under molestation and POCSO Act provisions. He has been remanded to judicial custody as investigations continue into the disturbing incident.

title: ‘Touched me 10 times’: Viral Kumbh girl files POCSO case against film director
content: Ernakulam Central police have registered a case against a film director under the Pocso Act following a complaint from the 'viral Kumbh Mela girl'. She alleges the director misbehaved with her when she was a minor, exploiting her for acting opportunities. The alleged incident occurred outside Kerala and the case will be transferred.

title: Can eating soybean cause breast cancer? Here's what experts say
content: Concerns about soy and breast cancer are largely unfounded, according to modern research.  Isoflavones in soy act differently than human estrogen, potentially reducing risk. Studies, especially from Asian countries, show no increased risk and even a possible decrease in cancer incidence and recurrence with moderate whole soy food consumption.

title: Air India may cut 100 flights daily as jet fuel prices hit; flights on these routes to be affected
content: ATF or jet fuel, for international carriers became costlier on Friday, with prices rising by 5 per cent. This marks the second consecutive monthly increase as oil marketing companies continue to gradually pass on the impact of higher global energy prices.

title: 'No strategic oil reserves like India': Pakistan admits vulnerability as fuel crisis deepens
content: Pakistan admits its vulnerability to global oil shocks, lacking strategic reserves like India, which holds 60-70 days of stock.  Islamabad's limited reserves leave it exposed, unlike India's stable prices aided by fiscal space and strategic stockpiles. Pakistan's IMF commitments restrict its ability to offer consumer relief amid soaring crude prices.

title: Gujarat Titans IPL playoff scenarios: GT locked in a tight race
content: Gujarat Titans reignited their IPL 2026 playoff hopes with a crucial four-wicket victory over Royal Challengers Bengaluru. Shubman Gill's explosive 43 and Rahul Tewatia's calm finish secured the chase.  With five games remaining and sitting fifth, GT needs at least two more wins to stay in contention for a playoff spot. With multiple teams clustered around the 12-point mark, they are firmly locked in a tight playoff race.

title: Russia test launches Soyuz-5 rocket with ‘world’s most powerful liquid-fuelled engine’
content: Russia's space program achieved a significant milestone with the successful maiden launch of its new Soyuz-5 rocket from Baikonur. This next-generation vehicle boasts powerful liquid-fuel engines, capable of carrying substantial payloads and promising reduced costs. Roscosmos hails it as a crucial step in modernizing Russia's space capabilities and maintaining global competitiveness.

title: Riyan Parag vape row: Ashwin says 'this could have been avoided'
content: Rajasthan Royals captain Riyan Parag was fined and received a demerit point for vaping in the dressing room during an IPL match. The incident drew criticism, with former India spinner Ravichandran Ashwin advising Parag to maintain personal space for his actions due to his leadership role.

title: What’s gone wrong? Bumrah’s disastrous IPL 2026 - 132 avg, 2 wkts in 8 games
content: Jasprit Bumrah is experiencing an uncharacteristic lean patch in IPL 2026, with his bowling average soaring to 132, the worst of his T20 career. Despite his historical dominance, he has struggled to take wickets and has conceded sixes to batsmen he previously contained. Experts cite a demanding international schedule and potential mental pressure as contributing factors to his current struggles.

title: MP boat tragedy: Mother, 3-yr-old son found holding each other in death
content: Rescue teams continue a relentless search at Bargi Dam after a tourist boat capsized. The ninth body, a mother holding her child, was recovered, deeply affecting rescuers. The vessel sank rapidly after a sudden storm. Survivors recount chaos and lack of safety measures. Efforts are underway to locate more missing individuals feared trapped inside the wreckage.

title: China: 14-year-old sentenced to life for rape and murder of classmate
content: A 14-year-old boy has been sentenced to life imprisonment for the rape and murder of his 15-year-old classmate. The Qujing Intermediate People’s Court found the defendant guilty of rape and intentional homicide. The court deemed the case "extremely serious" but could not impose the death penalty as the accused was under 18 at the time of the incident.

title: Gross GST collections hit record high in April despite US-Iran war concerns
content: According to government data released on Friday, gross GST revenue for the month reached a record Rs 2.43 lakh crore, registering an 8.7% increase over Rs 2.23 lakh crore collected in April last year.

title: India's World Cup stars needed a break before IPL 2026: Ian Bishop
content: Former West Indies pacer Ian Bishop suggests India's T20 World Cup winners might have benefited from a longer break before the IPL, citing their subsequent inconsistent performances. He also advocates for more varied pitches to balance the game between bat and ball, noting that strong bowling is crucial for champion teams.

title: Government hikes jet fuel prices by 5% for international airlines
content: Government has raised Aviation Turbine Fuel prices for international airlines by 5.33%, marking the second consecutive monthly increase due to the global energy crisis. This adjustment means foreign carriers will now pay market-linked rates for ATF. Domestic airlines, however, will continue to see moderated prices, with no change in their rates.

title: 'You'll be on the floor!' Furious Klaasen loses cool as fan films his family
content: Sunrisers Hyderabad star Heinrich Klaasen sternly warned a fan attempting to photograph him and his family. The incident, captured on video, saw Klaasen threaten to retaliate if the fan continued. This follows similar encounters with other IPL cricketers facing unsolicited photo requests.

title: 'King, Queen got me to do what no one else could': Trump removes tariffs on British whisky
content: In a significant move honoring King Charles III and Queen Camilla's state visit, US President Donald Trump has agreed to lift tariffs on British whisky exports. This decision, announced via Truth Social, aims to revive trade between Scotland and Kentucky, which had been impacted by the 10% levy.

title: Ultimate face-off: Can Starc stop Vaibhav Sooryavanshi's rampage?
content: A thrilling contest awaits as Vaibhav Sooryavanshi faces Mitchell Starc tonight.  The aggressive young batter, known for his explosive starts, takes on the seasoned Australian pacer returning from injury.  Sooryavanshi's remarkable strike rate against top bowlers sets the stage for a high-octane duel, with Starc aiming to curb his fearless approach.

title: Mamata storms EVM strongroom; TMC, BJP lock horns over tampering claims: How late-night drama unfolded
content: The Trinamool Congress staged a protest in Kolkata, alleging EVM tampering and procedural lapses in strongrooms before the West Bengal assembly polls. The party accused the BJP and Election Commission of collusion, while the EC asserted the strongrooms were secure and footage showed routine postal ballot segregation.

title: 'Continue seeing Gaza on YouTube': Netanyahu mocks flotilla as 20 ships with 175 activists sent back
content: Israel's Navy successfully intercepted a Gaza-bound flotilla carrying activists near Crete, preventing any vessels from reaching Palestinian shores. Prime Minister Netanyahu commended the operation, stating all participants were turned back. The US State Department backed Israel's actions, calling the flotilla a counterproductive stunt organized by a pro-Hamas entity.

title: Learn Marathi If You Live In Maharashtra, But No Violence: D Fadnavis
content: Mumbai, May 1 (IANS) Amid escalating debate over the mandatory use of the Marathi language in Maharashtra, Chief Minister Devendra Fadnavis on Friday emphasised that while pride in one's mother tongue is essential, the state government will not toler

title: Commercial LPG Prices Hiked By Rs 993 Amid War, Petrol, Diesel Rates Steady
content: Commercial LPG Prices Hiked By Rs 993 Amid Iran-US War, No Changes In Domestic Rates, Petrol, Diesel Prices

title: A Tale Of Two Countries: France's Nuclear Bet vs Germany's Renewable Push
content: France's decades-long investment in nuclear power now appears to be a strategic advantage. In Germany, fossil imports still play a central role.

title: US Eyeing $15 Million 'Dark Eagle' Hypersonic Strike Plan To Counter Iran
content: The objective behind the proposal is to enhance the US military's ability to strike Iranian ballistic missile launchers located deep inside the country.

title: In This Delhi Locality, Residents Open Taps Only To Find "Sewage-Like Water"
content: Residents say they have filed repeated complaints over the past year, but with little change on the ground

title: 700 Paramilitary Companies Deployed To Prevent Bengal Post-Poll Violence
content: Officials at the Election Commission headquarters will conduct real-time monitoring of the counting exercise through control rooms.

title: Jagan Reddy's Aide Arrested, Granted Bail And Re-Arrested In Multiple Cases
content: Pudi Srihari was first taken into custody and brought to Kuppam in Chittoor district on Thursday. After proceedings ended in the additional junior civil judge court, he got bail the same day

title: Suvendu Adhikari, The Student Who One-Upped The Master, And Is Trying Again
content: Adhikari joined Banerjee's Trinamool Congress in 1998 and entered the Bengal assembly as an MLA from the Kanthi Dakshin constituency in 2006.

title: US Expert Compares Pakistan's Oil Shock vs India's Fuel Stability Amid Iran War
content: Kiyosaki noted that India has widened its oil supply network over the years, leaning on Russia as a key source while bringing back shipments from Venezuela in 2026.

title: Opinion: Opinion | 35 US Lawmakers Just Moved A Bill That Could Upend Indian Techies' Careers
content: A group of Republicans wants to break the traditional F-1-to-H-1B-to-green-card path. The biggest casualty will be Indian students.

title: Stability In Northeast, Churn In South And A Knife-Edge Battle In Bengal: Exit Polls
content: Exit polls are not results. They set the narrative, not the final numbers. The real test will be on May 4.

title: India To See Above-Normal Rainfall In May, Warmer Nights: Weather Office
content: The India Meteorological Department said maximum temperatures are expected to remain normal to below normal across large parts of the country in May.

title: Kolkata EVM Strongrooms Fortified After Mamata Banerjee's Visit, Trinamool's Protests
content: The Kolkata Police's move follows high drama last night due to the Trinamool Congress's allegations of suspicious activities at counting centres

title: Centre Updates Citizenship Rules, Tightens Dual Passport Norms For Minors
content: The changes, known as the Citizenship (Amendment) Rules, 2026, introduce a slew of online initiatives for OCI cardholders, including a digital application and renunciation process.

title: New Lithium Reserves In US Can Power 500 Billion Phones, Reveals Study
content: The US Geological Survey stated in a new study that the Appalachians contain an estimated 2.3 million metric tons of undiscovered, economically recoverable lithium.

title: "Watch Gaza On YouTube": Netanyahu's Taunt As Israel Intercepts Aid Flotilla
content: Activists aboard the Global Sumud Flotilla said Israeli forces stormed their vessels overnight, smashing engines and detaining some of those onboard while they were sailing in international waters near Greece.

title: Bengaluru hotels to hike prices again after commercial gas cylinder price goes up from ₹2,162 to ₹3,152
content: The price of a commercial LPG cylinder has seen an unprecedented hike of over 70% in just 60 days in Bengaluru, with three hikes and a withdrawal of ₹150 discount during this period

title: Hit hard by ₹991 price hike for commercial LPG, Mysuru hoteliers say rate went up by ₹1,440 in two months
content: Describing the surge as ‘abnormal and alarming’, the Mysuru Hotel Owners’ Association said the sharp rise over just two months is placing mounting pressure on the hospitality industry and small businesses

title: Post-poll violence reported in three Bengal districts; police begin probe
content: According to party sources, Mr. Amin was assaulted at a tea stall by a group of local leaders allegedly led by a TMC regional functionary. Family members alleged that he had stepped out of his house at night when the incident took place

title: Bangladesh's foreign ministry summons India's acting Envoy over Himanta’s remarks: report
content: In a social media post, Himanta Sarma last week said 20 foreign nationals were apprehended in Assam and pushed back to Bangladesh

title: TVK election office set afire near Tiruchi
content: According to the police, the structure had been put up with coconut fronds and displayed party banners, serving as a local election office for the Srirangam Assembly constituency

title: Noida International airport to start commercial flights from June 15
content: On March 28, 2026, Prime Minister Narendra Modi formally inaugurated the National Capital Region’s second airport, the Noida International Airport

title: Modi empowered ED to cleanse cancer of financial crimes; not to target anyone: MoS Pankaj Chaudhary
content: He said the ED's name is "respected" not only in India but also at various global forums including the FATF which praised its investigative skills

title: Andhra University gets ₹180-crore C-ART observatory; monsoon below normal, says Earth Sciences Secretary
content: M. Ravichandran said El Niño conditions appeared to be developing earlier than expected, with potential impacts likely by July-August

title: India, UAE trade pact helps boost bilateral trade to cross $100 billion: Goyal
content: The Comprehensive Economic Partnership Agreement (CEPA) with the UAE acts as a gateway to Africa, other GCC and West Asian countries, CIS (Commonwealth of Independent States) countries and some European countries

title: T.N. elections: Hard work of DMK cadre will yield desired result on May 4, says Stalin
content: Mr. Stalin said on Labour Day that workers’ dignity, rights, and fair compensation remain central to the government’s approach

title: Naidu hands ₹5 lakh each to 35 construction workers in Pamarru on May Day, lists welfare schemes
content: The Chief Minister said the State government was spending more than ₹33,000 crore a year on pensions for 62.47 lakh beneficiaries, and another ₹615 crore on welfare for around 88 lakh unorganised workers

title: Anticipatory bail to Pawan Khera: Justice prevailed and arrest should be last resort, says Congress
content: The Supreme Court has granted Congress leader Pawan Khera anticipatory bail in a criminal case instituted on a complaint filed by Assam Chief Minister Himanta Biswa’s wife, saying the case appears to be motivated by political rivalry and does not warrant custodial interrogation

title: Above-normal heatwave days likely in some parts of India in May: IMD
content: In a statement, Director General of Meteorology, IMD, Mrutyunjay Mohapatra, said, "During May 2026, minimum temperatures are expected to be above normal across many parts of the country."

title: Hate speech: No cognisable offence made out against Anurag Thakur, Parvesh Verma, says Supreme Court
content: The high court had dismissed a petition by Brinda Karat and K M Tiwari challenging the trial court's refusal to direct registration of an FIR against Mr. Thakur and Mr. Verma for their alleged hate speeches over the anti-CAA protest at Delhi's Shaheen Bagh.

title: Cancel Labour Codes, fix ₹26,000 as minimum monthly wage: Left parties at Srikakulam May Day rally
content: CITU State secretary P. Tejeswara Rao said minimum wages had not been revised in 15 years, even as workers were being forced to put in 12-hour days for meagre pay

title: Elephant brought to temple turns violent, kills one, injures mahout
content: An elephant flipped over a car several times, destroying it, and also damaged a few two-wheelers present there, even as an Elephant Squad of the Forest Department tried to capture the jumbo before it moved out from the temple premises to a more populated area, says police

title: Allahabad HC dismisses plea seeking FIR against Rahul Gandhi over 2025 ‘fighting Indian State’ remark
content: The petitioner said the remark hurt public sentiments and amounted to a seditious and anti-national statement allegedly intended to destabilise the country

title: ‘Election Bill’: Rahul slams government on commercial LPG price hike
content: The Leader of Opposition in the Lok Sabha said he had warned that the heat of inflation would strike after the elections

title: Ladakhis proved to be first line of defence when country faced external threats: Amit Shah
content: The Union Home Minister inaugurated the sacred holy relics exposition of Tathagata Buddha and the 2569th Buddha Purnima celebrations in Leh during his two-day visit to Ladakh

title: This May Day reminds workers to be more organised, says Kerala Chief Minister
content: Kerala Chief Minister Pinarayi Vijayan said that even as neoliberal policies that undermine workers’ rights sweep across India, the voices of protest are emerging from the labour sector, which is very hopeful.

title: Rajasthan opens online self-enumeration for Census 2027, Governor urges participation
content: The process for "Census 2027" was formally launched in the State by Governor Haribhau Bagde at Lok Bhavan through self-enumeration

title: Kailash Mansarovar Yatra 2026: dates, routes, application process and all you need to know
content: Kailash Manasarovar Yatra 2026 isset for June–August, with applications open till May 19 and yatris to be selected via a fair, computerised draw

title: Make learning Kannada mandatory irrespective of board or curriculum followed: KDA report
content: It has been recommended that Kannada should be made compulsory in schools in Karnataka affiliated to any board, in keeping with the Kannada Language Learning Act, 2015.

title: Kshatriya Parishad pays tributes to P.V.G. Raju on birth anniversary in Vizianagaram
content: The MANSAS Trust founder and last king of the Vizianagaram princely State was remembered on Friday (May 1, 2026) for his contribution to the city’s education sector

title: Farooq Abdullah claims lack of support within INDIA bloc on restoration of J&K Statehood
content: National Conference president Farooq Abdullah urged the alliance to move beyond being a mere electoral pact, calling for more frequent meetings to address the "ignored" future of the nation.

title: Supreme Court reserves verdict on Pawan Khera’s plea for pre-arrest bail in Assam case
content: The case is ‘unprecedented’ with Chief Minister Himanta Biswa Sarma himself making statements against the Congress leader, senior advocate Abhishek Manu Singhvi tells the Supreme Court

title: Supreme Court grants pre-arrest bail to Pawan Khera in Assam case
content: SC says the cherished fundamental right of personal liberty cannot be jeopardised lightly, especially in the name of political rivalry

title: Bengal Minister Sujit Bose appears before ED for questioning in civic body recruitment scam
content: Mr. Bose is seeking re-election from the Bidhannagar seat, where polling happened in the final phase of the Assembly elections on April 29

title: Yellow alert for seven districts in Kerala on May 1
content: According to the weather bulletin issued by the IMD, a trough runs from South interior Karnataka to the Gulf of Mannar across Tamil Nadu at 0.9 km above mean sea level

title: CET re-test for students asked to remove janivara, hijab in Karnataka
content: Despite training staff on the dress code and instructing students with religious attire or identification to report at exam centres two hours early, the incidents occurred at three centres, according to the education minister

title: New Corporations, same old problems in Bengaluru
content: It has been eight months since the five corporations came into being in Bengaluru, in place of one large civic body. The number of wards have almost doubled from 198 to 369, expected to take governance closer to citizens. But has it really been the case?

title: Documentary on K.C. Venugopal reportedly the latest ‘casualty’ in the ‘tussle’ for post-poll UDF leadership
content: However, he added, media reports that the high command had issued an “extraordinary fiat” to stop the work on the documentary directed by Vinod Mankara had no factual basis. However, the AICC has issued a “general direction” requesting that the rank-and-file desist from promoting individual party leaders on social and conventional media.

title: Karnataka Cabinet approves Integrated Satellite Township project in Bengaluru South district at a cost of ₹18,133 crore
content: It agrees to provide a bank guarantee to a tune of ₹7,500 crore to the project that will entail acquisition of 7,404 acres in nine villages of Ramanagara taluk in Bengaluru South district

title: Silent on Ladakhi people's demands of Statehood, Sixth Schedule status: Congress slams Amit Shah
content: “The HM is in Ladakh today basking in the glory of the Piprahwa relics, while remaining silent on the demands of the people there for Statehood, Sixth Schedule status, and protection of land and employment," Jairam Ramesh said on X.

title: Bengaluru’s Bowring Hospital wall collapse leaves a trail of loss, pain, and grief
content: Seven persons were killed and seven others were injured after a portion of the compound wall of Bowring and Lady Curzon Hospital collapsed during heavy rain in Bengaluru.

title: Artificial fuel shortage at 56 of 4,312 petrol bunks in Andhra Pradesh, says BJP state chief Madhav
content: The State BJP chief urged petrol outlet owners to ensure uninterrupted supply and warned that shortages would hit agriculture, transport and emergency services

title: Hike in commercial LPG price: Shock and resignation on food street as LPG price goes up by 42%
content: The food kiosk is among the dozens of shacks and mulgis in the Yellareddyguda, Ameerpet and Sri Nagar Colony area with rows of hostels and PG accommodations where gig-economy workers, IT students and informal economy employees live

title: PM set to embark on 4-nation Europe trip next month; energy security in focus
content: The summit was to be held in May last year, but it was cancelled following the Pahalgam terror attack

title: Dijo Kappan, activist who championed consumer rights, passes away
content: A member of the Edamattam Kappil family of Pala, Kappan was active in student politics as a leader of the Kerala Student Congress, the campus wing of the Kerala Congress

title: PM Narendra Modi to visit Hyderabad on May 10, to launch ₹7,800-crore worth development initiatives in Telangana
content: Prime Minister to inaugurate the third railway line between Kazipet and Vijayawada, virtually inaugurate a major petroleum products terminal at Mallapur in Hyderabad

title: Karnataka government launches India’s first digital grievance redressal system for gig workers
content: The mechanism — a first of its kind in India — has been developed by the Karnataka Platform-based Gig Workers’ Board in collaboration with the Department of e-Governance

title: Bargi dam cruise boat tragedy: Death toll rises to nine; search operations continue
content: A cruise boat with 29 passengers and two crew members onboard overturned in the Bargi Dam after encountering a sudden storm

title: C.V. Anand takes charge as Telangana DGP
content: Mr. Anand began his policing career as Assistant Superintendent of Police in Warangal. Police officials said he spent the first nine years of his service in Naxal-affected districts.

content: The TOI Science Desk stands as an inquisitive team of journalists, ceaselessly delving into the realms of discovery to curate a captivating collection of news, features, and articles from the vast and ever-evolving world of science for the readers of The Times of India. Consider us your scientific companion, delivering a daily dose of wonder and enlightenment. Whether it's the intricacies of genetic engineering, the marvels of space exploration, or the latest in artificial intelligence, the TOI Science Desk ensures you stay connected to the pulse of the scientific world. At the TOI Science Desk, we are not just reporters; we are storytellers of scientific narratives. We are committed to demystifying the intricacies of science, making it accessible and engaging for readers of all backgrounds.
